questions.text: When you're dealing with a really big life decision — like whether to take a new job, move somewhere, make a major financial commitment, or something that could shift your whole trajectory — how do you actually go about making that call? Do you have a specific process ( pros/cons, sleeping on it, talking it through), or does it feel more like a gut thing? And once you've decided, do you ever doubt it or need to talk yourself into it, or are you pretty solid once you've made up your mind?

Beyond volleyball and fitness — what's the stuff you like to do with your hands or just for fun when you're not working or working out? Like, do you tinker with anything, build things, work on side projects, cook for the joy of it, go fishing, play an instrument, or just kind of mess around with random stuff? Is there anything you've getting into or want to get more into?

When you're injured, sick, or just not feeling great physically — how do you handle it? Do you push through, take complete rest, modify your activities, or do you have a specific threshold where you stop and recover? And when you do have to take it easy, does it mess with your head or are you pretty chill about it?

What's your vibe with news and staying informed about what's going on in the world? Are you someone who follows current events closely (news apps, newsletters, news shows), do you generally avoid the news, or are you somewhere in between where you catch things when they come up? Any specific topics you care about staying on top of, or do you feel like 'if it's important, I'll find out eventually'?

Have you done any estate planning, legal documents, or thought about what happens if something unexpected happens to you — like a will, power of attorney, healthcare directive, or nominating someone to make decisions for you? As someone living abroad and bouncing between countries, is this something you've thought about, or does it feel like 'not on my radar yet'?
